title: ‘Iran won’t leave Trump alone for killing Khamenei’: Ayatollah’s top aide Larijani warns
content: Iran's top security official has vowed retaliation against US President Trump for the killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei and over 1,000 Iranians.  Tehran launched missile strikes on Israel and US bases following the joint US-Israeli attacks. Trump dismissed the threats, calling for Iran's unconditional surrender, while Iran warned regional nations against aiding US operations.

title: Nitish Kumar's son Nishant enters politics, joins JD(U)
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar's son, Nishant Kumar, has formally joined the JD(U). This development follows Nitish Kumar's decision to step down as CM, sparking speculation about his successor and potentially ushering in a new political era in Bihar. Nitish Kumar himself announced his intention to contest the Rajya Sabha elections.

title: PM Modi inaugurates two new Delhi Metro corridors: Key routes and how they will help commuters
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has inaugurated and laid the foundation stone for infrastructure projects worth Rs 33,500 crore in Delhi. This includes two new Delhi Metro corridors, totaling over Rs 18,300 crore, aimed at enhancing public transport and connectivity. The event also features the foundation stone laying for three new metro routes under Phase V-A and GPRA redevelopment projects.

title: SA players slam ICC after England fly home while they remain stranded
content: Players from South Africa national cricket team and West Indies national cricket team criticised travel arrangements after their exit from the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026, questioning why England national cricket team flew home earlier. Quinton de Kock and David Miller voiced frustration, while Daren Sammy and Michael Vaughan also questioned the situation.

title: Pentagon vs Anthropic 'fight' may mean these new rules for all AI companies
content: The US government is reportedly preparing new AI regulations requiring companies to grant broad access to their models for federal contracts. This follows a dispute with Anthropic over military use, leading to contract cancellations and risk designations. The proposed guidelines also aim to prevent ideological bias in AI outputs and question compliance with non-US regulations.

title: Happy International Women's Day: 75+ messages, greetings, wishes and quotes
content: International Women's Day prompts reflection on the vital roles women play, often quietly.  The article highlights the significance of acknowledging their strength, effort, and presence.  It offers a collection of messages, wishes, and quotes to help express appreciation for the women who enrich our lives, encouraging a pause to recognize their impact.

title: TCS CEO says AI fears wiping billions off markets are a phase of 'worry and anxiety'
content: Global software stocks experienced an $830 billion wipeout following the launch of AI plugins, sparking fears of obsolescence. However, tech leaders like TCS CEO K Krithivasan push back, arguing complex enterprise systems won't be replaced by chatbots. Wall Street is repricing the sector, questioning if AI will hollow out or enhance software companies.

title: Punjab’s most-wanted gangster Amritpal Singh detained in Europe on Interpol red corner notice
content: Amritpal Singh, a key figure in the Jaggu Bhagwanpuria gang, has been detained on the Moldova border following an Interpol red corner notice. Punjab Police's newly formed Overseas Fugitive Tracking and Extradition Cell played a crucial role in his apprehension. Legal and diplomatic processes are now underway for his extradition to India to face serious charges.

title: Murder in Delhi's Uttam Nagar: MCD demolishes 'illegal portions' of accused's house
content: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi conducted a demolition drive, razing illegal portions of a house linked to an accused in the Uttam Nagar Holi clash. A 26-year-old man was killed in the dispute, which involved long-standing family feuds. Heavy police presence ensured order during the operation.

title: Mamata absent, no water in President’s washroom: Centre, Bengal spar over Murmu event
content: The Centre has demanded a response from the West Bengal government by Sunday evening regarding alleged protocol violations during President Droupadi Murmu's visit to state.  These alleged lapses include issues with venue arrangements, route cleanliness, and the absence of key state officials at her reception, prompting criticism from the Prime Minister.

title: Amazon sends letter to FCC saying: Reject application of Elon Musk's Spacex for Space data centers
content: Amazon's Project Kuiper has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's proposal for a million-satellite constellation, citing a lack of essential technical and orbital parameters. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, lacks space safety details, and would take centuries to deploy, potentially hoarding orbital resources.

title: Watch: MS Dhoni arrives in Ahmedabad for T20 World Cup final
content: MS Dhoni arrived in Ahmedabad ahead of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final between India and New Zealand national cricket team at Narendra Modi Stadium. Dhoni had earlier watched India’s semi-final win over England in Mumbai. Led by Suryakumar Yadav, India aim to defend their title, while New Zealand chase their maiden T20 World Cup crown.

title: ‘Mental harassment’: What YouTuber said days before crashing SUV on livestream
content: Motovlogger Anurag Dobhal, known as UK07 Rider, was injured in an SUV crash on the Delhi–Meerut Expressway while livestreaming at high speed. The incident occurred near Masuri, Ghaziabad, with over 80,000 viewers witnessing the accident. Dobhal, who had spoken of feeling lonely and emotionally overwhelmed moments before, suffered injuries and fractures but is in stable condition.

title: Explosives thrown outside New York mayor Zohran Mamdani's residence
content: Six individuals were arrested following violent clashes between far-right protesters and counter-protesters outside Gracie Mansion. One protester is accused of throwing smoking projectiles filled with nuts and bolts, prompting an investigation by the FBI and NYPD. The incident began with a demonstration organized by Jake Lang against the city's Muslim mayor.

title: Duel under the floodlights: India take on New Zealand for T20 World Cup title
content: Both are chasing history. No country has won the T20 World Cup consecutively, nor has a host ever won the tournament.

title: Failed Israeli mission to recover pilot’s remains leaves 41 dead in Lebanon
content: An Israeli special forces operation in Lebanon's Bekaa Valley, targeting Nabi Sheet, aimed to locate the remains of missing airman Ron Arad. The overnight raid, involving air strikes and ground clashes, resulted in 41 deaths and 40 injuries, according to Lebanese health officials. Israel confirmed the operation's failure to find Arad's remains but reiterated its commitment to finding missing servicemen.

title: Watch: First cricketer in 254 years! Bowler sets jaw-dropping world record
content: New Zealand bowler Brett Randell made history by becoming the first player in 254 years of first-class cricket to take five wickets in five consecutive deliveries. Playing for Central Districts against Northern Districts, he finished with 7/25, helping bowl out ND for 82 after CD’s 373 in the Plunket Shield match.

title: Dhurandhar 2’ update: Preview tickets soar and reach Rs 2400 sales run rampant
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' is generating immense buzz, with preview tickets fetching an astonishing Rs 2500. Advance sales have already surpassed 40,000 tickets, raking in approximately Rs 5 crore. The film's release across five languages on March 19 is anticipated to boost its performance, following the massive success of its predecessor.

title: 'Stabbed 40 times': 25-year-old engineer kills twin sister, attacks mother after argument
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Moradabad where a 25-year-old engineer allegedly murdered his twin sister, stabbing her multiple times, and attacked their mother. The accused, reportedly stressed over a breakup, lashed out when his sister tried to console him during an argument. Neighbours alerted police after hearing screams, leading to the swift arrest of the engineer.

title: 'If you kill Americans, we'll kill you': US releases montage of precision strikes
content: US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth issued a stark warning of lethal consequences for targeting Americans, amplified by CENTCOM on X. This declaration follows a joint US-Israel strike on Iranian territory that reportedly killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, leading to Iranian retaliatory strikes across the region. President Trump claimed significant neutralization of Iranian leadership and military capabilities.

title: IND vs NZ Final Match Timings: What time is India vs New Zealand T20 World Cup match?
content: India will face New Zealand in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final at Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday, starting at 7:00 PM IST. India aim for a record third title and to defend the trophy, while New Zealand national cricket team chase their maiden crown. Hot, dry weather and a batting-friendly pitch could make it a high-scoring contest.

title: Iran launches new solid-fuel missiles: What are they and what makes them different
content: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps launched the 27th wave of "Operation True Promise 4," utilising new solid-fuel Kheibar Shekan missiles targeting Israeli and American positions. These advanced missiles, capable of rapid deployment and difficult interception, underscore Iran's evolving missile capabilities amid escalating regional tensions.

title: President Murmu unhappy over venue shift, PM calls it 'insult'; Mamata hits back — Santal row
content: A political storm erupted after West Bengal's last-minute venue change for the International Santhal Conclave, drawing public displeasure from President Murmu. She questioned the reasoning and highlighted the community's struggle for recognition, sparking a fierce exchange between the TMC and BJP over alleged politicization of tribal issues and disrespect to the President's office.

title: 'Jhooth bol raha hai': SKY hits back at Santner's claim before final
content: A day before the T20 World Cup final, mind games have begun between India and New Zealand. Kiwi captain Mitchell Santner said restricting India to around 220 instead of 250 could help his team. India skipper Suryakumar Yadav jokingly dismissed the remark, saying “Jhooth bol raha hai,” adding India will adapt to the pitch rather than target a fixed total.

title: President Murmu, PM Modi hail 'nari shakti' on International Women's Day
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Droupadi Murmu extended heartfelt greetings on International Women's Day, celebrating 'Nari Shakti's' pivotal role in India's progress. Both leaders lauded women's determination, creativity, and zeal, emphasizing their empowerment as central to national development and the creation of an inclusive, prosperous society where every woman can achieve her full potential.

title: Blast at US embassy in Oslo: Explosion causes minor damage
content: An explosion struck the consular section entrance of the US Embassy in Oslo early Sunday, with police confirming no injuries were reported. The cause of the blast, which caused minor damage, is under investigation, though authorities believe it may have been an individual act. Investigations are ongoing, with a bomb squad deployed and a large security presence established.

title: Allen: 'Bat at No.1' instruction, MLC carnage and making of Kiwi destroyer
content: Finn Allen transformed from a middle-order batter into New Zealand’s explosive T20 opener after Wellington coach Glenn Pocknall promoted him to open in 2020. Recognising his power and attacking potential, Pocknall nurtured Allen’s aggressive style through challenging training. Allen later proved the decision right with record-breaking performances, including a 33-ball T20 World Cup century and a 151-run MLC blitz.

title: 'Used AI to find ways': Two college friends die by suicide inside Gujarat temple washroom
content: Two college friends, aged 18 and 20, were found dead in a temple washroom in Surat after allegedly injecting themselves with anesthetic drugs. Police discovered drug vials and syringes, along with mobile phone data indicating searches for suicide methods. The girls had left for college and did not return home.

title: 'Give them all': Sammy shares emotional post as WI stranded in India
content: West Indies head coach Daren Sammy expressed frustration after the team’s return from India following their exit from the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 was delayed due to Gulf airspace restrictions linked to the Israel–Iran conflict. Posting online, Sammy wrote, “I just wanna go home,” while Cricket West Indies said it is arranging safe travel for the squad.

title: UFC White House 2026 Fight: Ilia Topuria vs. Justin Gaethje to headline Freedom 250
content: The UFC has announced its historic UFC White House 2026 event, headlined by Ilia Topuria vs. Justin Gaethje for the undisputed lightweight championship. Taking place on June 14, 2026, on the White House South Lawn, the Freedom 250 card also features Alex Pereira vs. Ciryl Gane for the interim heavyweight title and several other high-profile bouts.

title: Delhi tourist spends night hiding in Nainital forest after rape bid by taxi driver
content: A woman's solo trip to Nainital turned terrifying when her taxi driver attempted to sexually assault her. She escaped into a forest, hiding overnight until rescued by villagers. The accused was arrested and confessed to the crime.

title: ‘Oil imports in full flow’: Hardeep Puri assures imports from non-Hormuz routes
content: India's oil imports are flowing smoothly through alternative routes, assuring citizens of uninterrupted energy supplies amid Middle East conflict. Minister Hardeep Singh Puri confirmed "full flow from all non-Hormuz routes," alleviating concerns about availability, affordability, and sustainability. Qatar has also assured immediate resumption of LNG supplies once the shipping route reopens.

title: Man held for strangling woman over marriage, money dispute; body dumped on Noida service road
content: A Delhi driver was arrested for allegedly strangling a woman to death with her scarf following a financial dispute. The victim, Kavita, met the driver, Vicky Mathur, and offered him money to marry her, but he refused. Mathur then allegedly strangled her, stole her cash, and dumped her body in Noida.

title: Boy goes on rampage at Karnataka school, attacks sleeping students with bed rod; 1 dead
content: A shocking incident unfolded at a Gurukul residential school in Ballari, Karnataka, where a boy allegedly attacked fellow students with a bed rod late at night. The assault resulted in one student's death and left eight others injured, with two sustaining serious wounds. All injured students were promptly taken to a hospital for medical attention.

title: Ravi Kishan reacts to Manoj Tiwari’s claim about bringing him into politics
content: During a lively segment on The Great Indian Kapil Show, Ravi Kishan cheekily responded to Manoj Tiwari's boast about introducing him to the political arena. With a glint in his eye, Kishan declared that he was the true mentor for both Tiwari and Nirahua, having taught them the very essentials like walking, speaking, and even crafting impactful lines.

title: Is US responsible for Iran school bombing that killed 160? What Trump said
content: President Trump declared the US has eliminated Iran's entire leadership, describing it as removing a "cancer." He asserted US forces have decimated Iran's navy, air force, and missile capabilities, claiming victory in the ongoing conflict. Trump denied US responsibility for a school bombing, blaming Iran's inaccuracy.

title: T20 World Cup 2026: Ahmedabad, the final, and the ghost of November 19 past
content: Silence and crowd noise were back in discussion ahead of Sunday's final but India captain Suryakumar Yadav chose to downplay the chatter. He acknowledged the challenges, pressure and excitement a final brings, and his message to the team was simple: be courageous.

title: Perennial dark horses, really? Why world keeps forgetting NZ’s World Cup pedigree
content: Nine ODI World Cup semi-finals. Two finals. Five T20 World Cup semi-finals and now a second final on Sunday. New Zealand’s record in big-ticket tournaments is anything but accidental, yet they continue to be casually described as cricket’s perennial dark horses.

title: Watch: Israel strikes several oil depots in Iran; towering smoke, massive blaze light up skyline
content: Israeli forces struck fuel storage complexes in Tehran and Karaj, causing large explosions and fires. The military stated these sites were used for Iran's armed forces, calling it a significant strike to dismantle military infrastructure. Iran's Revolutionary Guards Corps announced retaliatory attacks on Israel.

title: '80,000 watching live': Watch moment YouTuber crashes Fortuner on Delhi–Meerut Expressway
content: Motovlogger Anurag Dobhal, known as UK07 Rider, livestreamed his SUV crashing at high speed on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway. Visibly distressed and speaking of loneliness, he steered his vehicle into the highway divider.  Dobhal, who had spoken of personal and family disputes, sustained injuries and fractures but is stable.

title: PM Modi inaugurates two new Delhi Metro corridors
content: PM Modi inaugurated and laid the foundation stone of projects worth ₹15,200 crore under the central government's General Pool Residential Accommodation (GPRA) redevelopment plan

title: Indian Coast Guard evacuates rescued Indian fishers from Vietnam-flagged merchant ship off TN’s Colachel
content: The Coast Guard vessel docked at the Vizhinjam harbour in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala. Emergency responders rushed the rescued fishers to the Government Medical College for critical care

title: 'Grew up in a family where women were bosses': Rahul Gandhi tells students
content: "Women are generally more intelligent than men for the most part. Men are in a hurry and men get blinded by silly things. Women think longer term, they don't apply direct power they apply indirect power, it is more effective," he says.

title: Mammootty’s visit to Wayanad township for landslide-affected families sparks political controversy
content: A hot mic captured Mammootty asking CPI(M) district secretary, Wayanad, K. Rafeeq, not to tail him around the construction site; “Why are you always standing beside me? (Public) might think I have come at your (CPI(M)’s) behest”, he said

title: ED to challenge interim bail granted to Al Falah chairman
content: The money laundering case against Siddiqui and the varsity stems from two Delhi Police FIRs pertaining to alleged fraud with the students

title: Sri Lankan Tamil in Gummidipoondi rehabilitation camp murdered, six arrested
content: On the night of March 7, the victim was walking inside the camp when three persons who reportedly had personal enmity picked a quarrel with him and suddenly assaulted him with an iron rod which they had concealed.

title: Parandur airport project notified as ‘Special Project’
content: When projects require a single large parcel of land which includes water bodies, are notified as a special project, it would expedite the land acquisition and aid in the approval process.

title: Heroin worth ₹3.25 crore seized in Assam, five held
content: The CMO, in a post on X, said that acting on specific intelligence, the Cachar police intercepted the two vehicles on the Silchar-Kalain Road.

title: Ballari hostel rampage: Class 9 student attacks sleeping classmates; one dead, 7 injured
content: According to the police, the accused minor allegedly attacked his classmates with an iron bed rod following a minor dispute after dinner, when the victims were reportedly asleep.

title: Tiruchi Police announce traffic diversions in view of DMK State conference on March 9
content: The diversion will come into effect from 6 a.m. on March 9. DMK president and Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin will preside over the conference.

title: Five-year-old boy abducted in U.P.'s Prayagraj, search on
content: The boy was allegedly abducted on March 7 in Sarai Kalan village when the child had just returned home from school

title: Protocol lapses at President event: Centre seeks response from West Bengal Government
content: President Droupadi Murmu on March 7 expressed disappointment with the West Bengal Government for changing the venue of the International Santal Conference in Darjeeling district and not adhering to the desired protocol to receive her at the event

title: Any disrespect to President Murmu deeply regrettable, unacceptable: Edappadi Palaniswami
content: On March 7, President Droupadi Murmu expressed disappointment with the West Bengal government for changing the venue of the International Santal Conference in Darjeeling district and not adhering to the desired protocol to receive her at the event.

title: Nishant Kumar, son of Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, officially joins JD-U
content: JD(U) party working president Sanjay Kumar Jha formally welcomed Nishant Kumar by giving him primary membership of the party

title: 'Lapses' at Prez event: Mayawati urges respect for President's office
content: Everyone must respect the office of the President in accordance with the ideals and dignity of the Constitution and avoid politicising constitutional positions in any manner, says BSP chief Mayawati

title: West Asia conflict: How an Indian in UAE opened the doors of his farm for stranded Indians
content: The initiative was organised by Dhiraj Jain, a businessman based in the UAE, who offered his property to those left stranded

title: International Women’s Day: Educated, empowered women pillars of progressive nation, says Droupadi Murmu
content: As 'Nari Shakti' continues to excel across diverse fields and lead with courage, it strengthens the foundations of a more inclusive and prosperous society, she said

title: In Chennai, a former Commissioner’s name lives on, in different forms
content: Nearly ten years since historian V. Sriram pointed out that two road name boards would cause Sir Eric Conran-Smith to turn in his grave, those twin errors persist

title: Mercury rises across north; Delhi records highest maximum temperature for first week of March in 50 years
content: According to weather data from the past 50 years, earlier the highest maximum temperature reading during the first seven days of March at Safdarjung, the city's base weather station, was 34.8°C logged on March 5, 1999.

title: 45% Kashmir students up to Class 12 report sub-optimal mental well-being due to social media: study
content: “The inability of some students to check in with some technologies according to their wishes results in a feeling of anxiety. More than 14% were moderately to highly anxious when they were unable to use text messages,” according to the study, published this year by the Ianna Journal of Interdisciplinary Studies

title: Delhi court grants two-week interim bail to Al-Falah chairman Jawed Siddiqui in money laundering case
content: “After considering all the present facts and circumstances, I am of the considered view that the applicant/ accused deserves to be enlarged on interim bail as the wife of applicant/ accused is unwell,” the court said in its order

title: Why India’s ‘leaky pipeline’ in research is unlike the rest of the world
content: Most women PhD holders in STEM in India find themselves unable to access long-term, lucrative, and prestigious research jobs

title: Delhi woman hides in Uttarakhand forest overnight to escape rape attempt by taxi driver
content: To save herself, the woman ran towards the woods and hid in the forest overnight. She somehow managed to reach Patwa Dangar village on Friday (March 6) morning, and with the help of locals, filed a police report.

title: Many colorectal cancer patients in India diagnosed at advanced stage, finds Kidwai study
content: Doctors urge people not to ignore warning signs such as persistent abdominal pain, changes in bowel habits, blood in the stool, unexplained weight loss or prolonged fatigue

title: Onwards and upwards: women of Tamil Nadu claim their space in local administration
content: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1992 mandates at least 33% (one-third) reservation for women in Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) across India, including for positions of chairpersons. The HinduC. Palanivel Rajan takes a deep dive to examine whether women have moved from being seen as “proxies” or “rubber-stamp” heads to achieving true empowerment. Hearteningly, hewe find that many women leaders have indeed wrested agency and truereal power.

title: West Asia conflict: Over 52,000 Indians returned between March 1-7 after partial opening of airspace
content: The MEA said Indian missions in the region have issued detailed advisories and set up 24x7 helplines to assist Indians; more flights planned in coming days

title: ‘Cannot be dismissed as logistical issues’: Odisha CM expresses anguish over shifting of tribal meet venue in Bengal
content: CM Mohan Majhi’s reaction came close on the heels of rare public displeasure expressed by President Droupadi Murmu over shifting of venue for the International Santhal Conclave in Darjeeling

title: Indian students stuck in Iran fear for safety; families plead for evacuation
content: Places in Iran to which they are being relocated are no longer safe, bringing them back to India is the only solution, says AIMSA; evacuation via Armenia being considered from where students could ‘arrange and fund their return’ to India

title: New Search For Flight MH370 Ends With No Clues To 12-Year Mystery
content: The Boeing 777 carrying 239 people vanished from radar screens on March 8, 2014, while en route from Kuala Lumpur to Beijing.

title: Israel Aims To Weaken Iran Regime For Internal Change As War Enters Day 9
content: As the war between Israel and Iran enters its ninth day with intense aerial strikes and mounting regional tensions, Israel's objective is to weaken the Iranian regime to a point where internal change becomes possible.

title: UAE Advises Citizens Against Filming Incident Sites, Prohibited Areas
content: The authorities in the United Arab Emirates have issued a warning that strict action will be taken against anyone who films incident sites, prohibited areas, or airport interiors without authorisation.

title: From 'Permitting' To 'Partnering', US Shifts Energy Language Amid Gulf Crisis
content: As the Iran-Israel war in the Middle East squeezes global oil supply, the Trump administration hosted Latin American leaders and the US treasury secretary pivoted from a tone of authority to one of partnership - a shift in language.

title: Wave Of US Court Rulings Frees Indian Migrants From Detention
content: A wave of federal court rulings across the United States this week has ordered the release or bond hearings for at least 15 Indian nationals held in immigration detention.

title: RM Nachammai Becomes First Woman At Helm Of Functional Nuclear Power Plant
content: In a landmark moment for India's nuclear power program, RM Nachammai has created history by becoming the first woman chief superintendent looking after the operations of a functional nuclear power plant.

title: US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran President Says "Will Be Forced To Respond" If Attacked By Neighbours
content: The war in the Middle East was triggered after the US and Israel attacked Iran last Saturday, killing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

title: Khamenei's Top Aide Warns 'Won't Leave Trump Alone'. He Responds
content: The US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on February 28, killing its 86-year-old supreme leader Ali Khamenei and triggering a war in the Middle East.

title: "Not Helping Them": Trump On Russia Reportedly Sharing Intel With Iran Amid War
content: Trump also waved off a question about how Russia assisting Iran in such a way might affect his view of the US-Russia relationship.

title: Iranian Sailor Told Father Of US Ultimatum Before Torpedo Attack: Report
content: A sailor of the Iranian Navy warship IRIS Dena had phoned his father minutes before the frigate was struck by a torpedo, saying American forces had twice ordered the crew to abandon the warship, according to a report.

title: Delhi Woman, On Solo Trip, Hides In Nainital Forest Overnight To Escape Rape Attempt
content: A solo trip turned into a night of terror for a woman from Delhi who spent the entire night hiding in a forest in Uttarakhand, injured and with no mobile phone, to escape an allegedly rape attempt by her taxi driver.

title: 52,000 Indians Returned From Middle East After Airspace Reopened Partially
content: India said it is continuously monitoring the evolving situation in West Asia, especially in the context of the safety of its citizens stranded in the region.

title: Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahalvi Says Democracy Within Reach
content: Iran's exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi has said the country may be nearing a political turning point, telling CBS's 60 Minutes that "this is our chance now" as he outlined a plan for democratic transition after decades of clerical rule.

title: Man Who Accused Shankaracharya Of Child Abuse Attacked During Train Journey
content: Ashutosh Brahmachari, who has filed a child abuse case against religious leader Avimukteshwarananda, has filed a fresh case, alleging that he was attacked on a train journey to Prayagraj

title: Case Against Anil Ambani, Reliance Communications In Rs 1,085 Crore Bank Fraud
content: CBI has registered a case against industrialist Anil Ambani, his company Reliance Communications Limited, and its former director Manjari Ashok Kacker in connection with an alleged bank fraud case involving more than Rs 1,085 crore.

title: 'No Water In Washroom, Garbage On Road': Centre vs Bengal Over President Trip
content: West Bengal Chief Minister and Trinamool Congress leader Mamata Banerjee has accused the ruling BJP at the Centre of disrespecting and misusing the highest chair in the country for its agenda after President Droupadi Murmu expressed displeasure

title: Second Bulk Carrier Claiming To Be Chinese Passes Through Strait Of Hormuz
content: Another bulk carrier signaled it was Chinese-owned as it sailed through the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow waterway at the mouth of the Persian Gulf that's been effectively closed for a week due to multiple attacks in the area.

title: Kuwait Airport Targeted, Saudi Intercepts Drones As Iran Continues Attacks
content: Fuel tanks at the Kuwait airport were hit while 15 drones were intercepted and destroyed in Saudi Arabia as Iran continued attacks a day after its President Masoud Pezkeshian said Tehran will not target neighbouring countries.

title: TNExpat platform launched to provide expats a peek into life, culture in T.N.
content: Tamil Nadu Industries Minister T.R.B. Rajaa inaugurates the platform; the website is currently available in five languages, making it accessible to a wider international community

title: Tamil Nadu releases Government Order to regulate cosmetology, aesthetics, and hair transplant clinics
content: The order is based on the National Medical Commission guidelines on aesthetics and hair transplant issued on September 20, 2022, which have been incorporated into Tamil Nadu Clinical Establishment rules

title: There is no repugnancy between provisions of RERA and Karnataka Apartment Ownership Act: Karnataka High Court
content: ‘Association of owners formed under KAOA is the right body to manage residential apartments, not cooperative societies’

title: Rewriting Kerala-Africa migration narratives: towards a women’s gaze
content: Africa has lived in Kerala’s imagination through stories of trade, migration, adventure, and opportunity. However, most of these accounts were written through a male gaze. Now, women travellers are rewriting these narratives

title: Young women duo rev up a four-decade-old family garage in Kottayam
content: Amritha and Shruthimol have been working at ‘Ringoos Auto Works’ at Ponknnum town in  Kottayam, owned by Sivadasan, their father-in-law, for over three years now

title: Women entrepreneurs face disparity in fundraising
content: ‘Women founders continue to build resilient, high-potential businesses, yet the share of capital reaching them remains disproportionately small’

title: Rahul Gandhi offers five guarantees to people of Kerala ahead of Assembly polls
content: The five ‘welfare guarantees’  were aimed at giving a social security cover for the people of Kerala, especially the women and the youth, says Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha. Increase in welfare pensions for the marginalised, free travel for women on KSRTC buses are some of the guarantees

title: Iran-Israel conflict: CBSE postpones Class 12 board exam in West Asia
content: The board has cancelled Class 10 exams till March 11 and said the mode of declaration of results will be announced separately

title: Vijay promises ₹2,500 monthly assistance for women, free bus travel
content: Speaking at the International Women’s Day celebrations organised by the party in Mamallapuram, he hits out at the ruling party and claims that 30% of the budgetary allocation in T.N. is being siphoned off for the Chief Minister and his Cabinet

title: Maharashtra Government inks MoU with Finnish firms to develop Sassoon Dock
content: Sassoon Dock records an average annual fish production of 50,000 to 60,000 metric tonnes, making it a priority for the Maharashtra government, State Fisheries Minister Nitesh Rane said

title: Since 2017, cases of SCs denied access to public spaces on the rise with lion’s share in U.P.
content: From the latest available set of crime data in the NCRB’s 2023 Crime in India report, there were 180 reported cases of SCs being denied access to public spaces under the Prevention of Atrocities (SC/ST) Act across the country. Of these, 173 cases were reported in U.P. alone

title: Manipur BJP MLA’s burial yet to take place, 15 days after death
content: Vungzagin Valte was left paralysed by a mob attack in Imphal in May 2023; Before conducting last rites, Valte family, supporters demand NIA probe into the assault, and a political solution to the conflict

title: Six wagons of coal-laden goods train derail in MP’s Katni, distrupting rail traffic
content: Railway officials and staff rushed to the spot after receiving information and began assessing the situation

title: Kerala govt. brings out draft notification revising minimum wages of employees in State’s private health sector
content: The proposed revised wages sits lower than the basic pay hike being demanded by a large section nurses in the State

title: Intruders will be driven out from Kedarnath to Kanyakumari, says Amit Shah
content: Says names of those who are not Indian citizens must be removed from the voter list, as a clean electoral roll is essential for safeguarding democracy

title: Govt. mulling deploying Indian Navy to escort ships stranded in Persian Gulf: Govt. source
content: Decision likely in two days; govt source says Iranian promise of not attacking neighbours may facilitate ship movement across Strait of Hormuz

title: LPG hike: Home cooking and eating out expected to cost more
content: Bangalore Hotels Association says the Union government has recently also stopped the discount on MRP for bulk buyers of commercial LPG cylinders

title: Kerala Chief Minister dedicates State’s longest backwater bridge
content: The Perumbalam bridge in Alappuzha ends the long wait for a direct road link for Perumbalam islanders to the mainland

title: Operation Cy-Hunt 2.0: 46 cases over cyber financial fraud booked, 26 arrested in district in a day
content: While the Ernakulam Rural Police arrested 16 alleged offenders, the Kochi City Police nabbed 10 accused during the day-long drive

title: Women lead the way at this century-old school in Bengaluru
content: A symbol of empowerment at a time when women’s education was rare and even resisted, Mahila Seva Samaja stands tall today, run by an all-women management

title: Coimbatore gang rape case: convicted trio carried spare clothes to change attire
content: The three men used to steal clothes that are hung outside houses for drying. They would keep a shirt inside the ones they wear as a replacement for a change in the attire, in the event of committing an offence, said a police officer.

title: Plea for CM’s intervention to ensure rapid-acting insulin for children under Mittayi
content: Type One Diabetes Foundation (Kerala) alleges that the insulin currently provided is causing health problems among children

title: Normalcy returns to Kashmir after six days; DGP says sustained pressure put on anti-national elements
content: Concertina wires placed on roads and interior lanes in parts of Srinagar city, Budgam and Baramulla were also removed, allowing vehicular traffic to ply smoothly on the roads; locals said internet speed, which was reduced to 2G service, and prepaid mobile calling were also restored

title: No foreign capital should dictate New Delhi: Farooq Abdullah
content: Commenting on U.S. announcing a ‘waiver’ to India for importing Russian oil, the National Conference leader states that India is a sovereign country and does not need anyone’s permission

title: Only details of 650 internal complaint committees of Dakshina Kannada uploaded on SHe-Box portal: Karnataka State Commission for Women chairperson
content: Nagalakshmi Choudhary says setting up of Internal Complaint Committees at workplaces is mandatory as per the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act 2013

title: Education is key to women’s empowerment, says A.P. Governor
content: Governor Syed Abdul Nazeer gives away degrees to 2,591 students at 22nd convocation at SPMVV in Tirupati; DRDO former scientist Sasikala Sinha conferred with honorary doctorate

title: Share of women-led start-ups in ELEVATE sees steady rise
content: The percentage of women-led start-ups in the programme has more than doubled since the launch of the programme in 2017

title: CPI(M) stages protest against State Budget in Kalaburagi
content: ₹4.48 lakh crore State Budget, presented as a pro-people Budget aimed at the development of all sections, in reality favours private and corporate interests, says CPI(M) district secretary Neela

title: E-health system launched in Edamalakkudy health centre
content: patients from Edamalakkudy can now schedule online appointments at the Adimaly Taluk Hospital, the District Hospital, or the Medical College Hospital

title: CBI initiates probe into alleged tampering of BSF records to falsely implicate two constables in smuggling case
content: In its order dated September 8, 2025, the Delhi High Court has said there was ‘sufficient material to reach a prima facie conclusion’ that an in-depth probe was required into the matter

title: Samrat Choudhary, top prospect for Bihar’s next CM, to meet Amit Shah in West Bengal on March 8
content: Duo likely to address a public meeting together in Bagdogra; RJD’s Rabri Devi says Nitish Kumar should not leave Bihar; Nishant Kumar meets JD(U) legislators on the eve of formally joining the party

title: LB Mack returning to Chargers on one-year deal
content: Nine-time Pro Bowler Khalil Mack reached a one-year deal to remain with the Chargers, with sources telling ESPN it's worth $18 million.

title: Skubal fans five, allows leadoff HR as U.S. wins
content: Great Britain's Nate Eaton greeted Team USA pitcher Tarik Skubal with a home run on the first pitch of the game Saturday, as the defending AL Cy Young Award winner made his only start of the World Baseball Classic in Houston in a 9-1 U.S. win.

title: Hurley tossed, fined as UConn falls to Marquette
content: UConn coach Danny Hurley was ejected in the final second as Marquette hung on for a 68-62 victory that prevented the fourth-ranked Huskies from winning a share of the Big East regular-season title and hurt their hopes for a 1-seed in the NCAA tournament.

title: Messi paid $70M-$80M per year, Miami owner says
content: Inter Miami co-owner Jorge Mas revealed that captain Lionel Messi is paid between $70 million and $80 million a year, including the player's ownership shares.

title: A's prospect Hernaiz caps Puerto Rico walk-off win
content: Darell Hernaiz, 24, an infield prospect with the Athletics, hit a 374-foot home run to left field in the 10th inning Saturday, lifting host Puerto Rico to a 4-3 World Baseball Classic victory over Panama at Hiram Bithorn Stadium in San Juan.

title: Topuria-Gaethje, Pereira-Gane top UFC's WH card
content: A lightweight title bout between Ilia Topuria and Justin Gaethje will headline the UFC's White House event on June 14, while Alex Pereira and Ciryl Gane will vie for the interim heavyweight championship, the promotion announced Saturday.

title: Crosby bids adieu to Vegas, excited about Ravens
content: Longtime Raiders star Maxx Crosby, who was traded to the Ravens on Friday, expressed his appreciation for his time with Mark Davis' franchise in a video published to his social media accounts Saturday.

title: UTEP fan's clash with JSU players under review
content: The University of Texas-El Paso, Jacksonville State and Conference USA will review an altercation that appeared to involve a UTEP fan picking up a chair in a fiery exchange with Jacksonville State players on Saturday.

title: Women's Bracketology: No. 1 seeds set, but bubble is toughest in years
content: Texas and South Carolina are locks for 1-seeds. TCU replaces Michigan as the final 2-seed. The bubble? Wide open.

title: Solak: Why Crosby fits perfectly with the Ravens' D -- and their Super Bowl window
content: Baltimore had to give up two first-round picks to get him, but Crosby can dominate in the Ravens' defense.

content: George Russell, Kimi Antonelli, Charles Leclerc finished atop the podium at the first race of the 2026 F1 season.

title: How Duke's Krzyzewskiville ordering test decides who gets the best 'Cameron Crazies' seats
content: There aren't many tests at Duke that will produce a median score among takers of 43.2%. But Krzyzewskiville's annual "ordering test" isn't just any test.

content: Insurance controversy rocked the roster, but the home team in Pool A of the World Baseball Classic is writing its next chapter.
